description:
You are a system administrator who wants a quick daily overview of how many failed SSH-login attempts occurred on your server.  
An example authentication log has already been placed at  

    /home/user/logs/auth.log  

Your job:

1. Read /home/user/logs/auth.log, count how many times the exact phrase  

       Failed password  

   appears for each distinct calendar day (using the date that is shown at the
   beginning of every log line).

2. Create a new summary file  

       /home/user/logs/failed_ssh_summary.log  

   with one line per day, sorted in ascending (oldest-to-newest) date order.  

   REQUIRED output format of each line (the automated checker depends on this
   exact syntax—no extra spaces, no blank lines):

       YYYY-MM-DD|ATTEMPT_COUNT

   where  
   • YYYY is four-digit year,  
   • MM is two-digit month,  
   • DD is two-digit day,  
   • ATTEMPT_COUNT is the decimal count for that day.

   Example of the correct format (do not hard-code these numbers, they are only
   illustrative):

       2025-01-10|17
       2025-01-11|23

3. Make sure /home/user/logs/failed_ssh_summary.log is world-readable
   (chmod 644 or equivalent) so that automated tests can open it.

Only standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities that are normally present in a
base distribution (e.g., grep, awk, sort, uniq, cut, wc, etc.) may be used.
Because the task is simple, it can be completed with roughly 2-3 pipeline
commands, but feel free to experiment in the shell as you work.

When done, the automated test will:
• Check that /home/user/logs/failed_ssh_summary.log exists.
• Verify that its permissions allow read access.
• Confirm that its contents match the required YYYY-MM-DD|COUNT lines exactly.

No other files should be modified or created inside /home/user/logs.
truth:
Environment before the agent starts:

1. Directory /home/user/logs already exists and is writable by the normal user.

2. File /home/user/logs/auth.log already exists with the **exact** 5 lines
   shown below (no trailing blank lines):

Jan 10 2025 08:23:45 server sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 54321 ssh2
Jan 10 2025 09:15:02 server sshd[23456]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.5 port 54433 ssh2
Jan 11 2025 10:00:00 server sshd[34567]: Failed password for user john from 198.51.100.7 port 60000 ssh2
Jan 11 2025 10:05:25 server sshd[34568]: Failed password for user mike from 198.51.100.8 port 60001 ssh2
Jan 11 2025 23:59:59 server sshd[40000]: Failed password for invalid user test from 198.51.100.9 port 60002 ssh2

3. No other files exist in /home/user/logs.

Expected state after successful completion:

1. File /home/user/logs/failed_ssh_summary.log exists and is **world-readable**
   (octal mode 644 or more permissive).

2. /home/user/logs/failed_ssh_summary.log contains exactly the following two
   lines (ending with a single trailing newline):

2025-01-10|2
2025-01-11|3

3. No additional files are created inside /home/user/logs and /home/user/auth.log
   remains unchanged.
